mechanism_narrative: Higher federal funds rates increase discount rates for equity valuations, directly depressing S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower when FEDFUNDS rises.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden jump in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals labor market deterioration and reduced consumer spending, triggering immediate negative equity repricing.
failure_condition: SPX forward 5d returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE crosses 4.5%.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized consumer and business demand growth, driving positive equity returns over a quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce positive 60d SPX returns in >60% of cases.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production growth above its 12-month moving average signals capacity utilization tightening and corporate earnings expansion, boosting equity valuations.
failure_condition: Positive INDPRO growth correlates with negative 20d SPX returns in >50% of observations during mid-cycle phases.

mechanism_narrative: Rising policy rates combined with falling unemployment indicate an overheating economy where monetary tightening outpaces labor slack, forcing equity valuation compression.
failure_condition: The joint signal of rising FEDFUNDS and falling UNRATE yields positive 20d SPX returns in >65% of triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2% reflects robust aggregate demand that supports corporate revenue forecasts, generating positive short-term equity momentum.
failure_condition: GDPC1 growth > 2% is followed by negative 5d SPX returns in >55% of quarterly release windows.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation readings above 3.0% force central banks to maintain restrictive policy longer, increasing discount rates and depressing long-horizon equity returns.
failure_condition: CPIAUCSL above 3.0% coincides with positive 60d SPX returns in >60% of monthly observations.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment growth exceeding 200,000 monthly indicates strong labor demand that supports wage growth and consumer spending, lifting equity prices.
failure_condition: PAYEMS above 200k yields negative 20d SPX returns in >50% of cases during early-cycle recovery phases.
